summary.intent: Investigate and implement a new `moltis-crm` crate scaffold (CHA-8) in a Rust workspace, following established crate patterns for CRM domain functionality.
summary.outcome: Successfully created the full `crates/crm/` scaffold with 8 new files and 4 modified workspace integration files, passing all quality checks (cargo check, 18/18 tests, clippy, nightly rustfmt), and committed to a feature branch ready for PR.

repo: Standard crate scaffold pattern: lib.rs, error.rs, types.rs, store.rs (trait), store_sqlite.rs, store_memory.rs, migrations/ — derived from moltis-projects as the canonical reference
SQLite store implementations use `sqlx::query_as::<_, Row>` and `sqlx::query` (non-macro) — not `sqlx::query!` macro — to avoid requiring offline query cache files
Workspace lint `unwrap_used = "deny"` applies globally; test modules must explicitly allow it with `#[allow(clippy::unwrap_used, clippy::expect_used)]`
New crates must be added to both `members` and `default-members` in root Cargo.toml, plus workspace `[dependencies]`
Gateway features are optional dependencies feature-gated; CLI enables them via `default` feature list forwarding gateway features
Migration calls are added to `gateway/src/server.rs` wrapped in `#[cfg(feature = "crm")]` blocks alongside existing migration calls
Nightly rustfmt is used (not stable) — requires `cargo +nightly fmt` for formatting

code.finding: Reference implementation for SQLite store pattern — uses query_as::<_, Row> with manual FromRow, not query! macro
Migration calls are feature-gated with #[cfg(feature = "...")]; new crates append their run_migrations() call here
Had to rewrite from sqlx::query! macro to sqlx::query_as::<_, Row> after initial compile failure due to missing offline query cache

workflow: Linear API requires GraphQL calls; LINEAR_API_KEY env var was not available in the initial sandbox context and had to be retrieved via MCP context-mode plugin
MCP ctx_batch_execute can time out on large batches; falling back to targeted individual reads is more reliable
Running `cargo check` on the individual crate first (not the whole workspace) speeds up the feedback loop during scaffolding
Background bash tasks via run_in_background allow gateway compile checks to run in parallel with other work

friction: LINEAR_API_KEY was not available as an environment variable in the sandbox — required multiple attempts and workarounds via MCP context-mode plugin to post Linear comments
MCP ctx_batch_execute timed out on the first large batch read, requiring a fallback to sequential targeted reads
Initial store_sqlite.rs used sqlx::query! macros which fail without an offline query cache — had to rewrite to use non-macro query_as::<_, Row> pattern after compile failure
Clippy unwrap_used lint fired on test code, requiring a second pass to add allow attributes to all test modules
open_items: Typed `ChannelType` reference for Contact.source field deferred — currently string-based
Tags, notes, and interaction history for Contact explicitly excluded from P1.1 — deferred to subsequent phases
No CRM planning docs exist in plans/ — domain model decisions are intentionally minimal pending further design
Gateway and CLI feature wiring is in place but no actual CRM API routes or handlers were created — scaffold only
PR creation was not captured in the transcript — may still need to be opened against the feature branch
